text: For coupled Burgers-swept-KdV system (u_t + 3 u u_x = -d_x(3 v^2 + v_xx); v_t + 6 v v_x + v_xxx = -d_x(u v)) with v(x,0)=2 sech^2(x+5) and u(x,0)=0.5 v^2 + 0.2 v on [-15,15], Nx=256, T=8.0: does the v soliton survive (single dominant peak with amplitude >= 1.0, mass drift < 8%, |u|,|v| < 15)?

bank_use_rationale: NoKB condition (no bank). Following progressive-complexity: spectral is the simplest method that can resolve v_xxx accurately on Nx=256 (FD-2 has ~1e-2 relative error on v_xxx for this resolution which corrupts dispersion). Paired with simplest time integrator (explicit RK4) and NO dealiasing. dt set by RK4 imag-axis CFL on linear KdV term i*k^3.

rationale: Classic aliasing blowup. The pseudospectral method without dealiasing aliases the quadratic and bilinear nonlinearities (3 v^2, u v, u u_x, v v_x) onto low modes, depositing spurious energy at high k. With v_xxx providing fast linear oscillations at large k, the aliased energy at high k amplifies catastrophically. By t=0.4 |u|max is already 4.1 (initial 2.4), and overflow occurs at t=0.5. The growth pattern (rapid energy accumulation at high k then NaN) is the textbook signature of aliasing instability for nonlinear spectral methods.

Add 2/3-rule dealiasing — single-component upgrade that directly targets the diagnosed aliasing failure mode without touching time integrator or other components. Keep RK4 and dt unchanged so any subsequent failure isolates aliasing as the sole cause if E2 succeeds, or rules it out if E2 still fails.

method: Fourier pseudospectral WITH 2/3-rule dealiasing + explicit RK4
bank_use_rationale: NoKB. Single-component upgrade: add 2/3 dealiasing mask applied to FFTs feeding derivative operators AND to nonlinear products (v^2, u^2, u*v) prior to their derivative. Time integrator and dt unchanged. This isolates whether aliasing alone is the failure cause.

rationale: Dealiasing fixed the aliasing blowup. Simulation reaches T=8 with mass(v) conserved to machine precision. However: (a) v peak amplitude is 1.50 (>= 1.0 threshold OK), but v has fragmented into ~4 dominant peaks of comparable amplitude (1.50, 1.38, 1.16, 1.18) — NOT cleanly a single dominant peak. (b) |u|max grows monotonically and reaches 15.88, just barely exceeding the |u|<15 bound. u-equation is Burgers-like without dissipation, naturally develops steepening structures driven by source -d_x(3 v^2 + v_xx). Both issues suggest small-scale dispersive noise / under-resolved Burgers shocks contribute. Phenomenon target NOT cleanly met: |u| bound violated.

Two diagnosed issues: (1) multi-peak v structure suggesting accumulated small-scale dispersive noise and (2) u steepening hitting |u|=15.88. Add ONE component: a mild exponential spectral filter applied to BOTH u and v at end of each step. The filter attenuates the top ~45% of k-modes (above k_filter = 0.55 k_max) with a smooth exponential decay. This is a single-component upgrade: it adds spectral filtering (a form of weak hyperviscosity) on top of the existing dealiasing + RK4. It directly addresses small-scale noise that the 2/3 mask does not damp.

method: Fourier pseudospectral + 2/3 dealiasing + exponential spectral filter (alpha=36, p=18 above |k|=0.55 k_max) + explicit RK4
bank_use_rationale: NoKB. Single-component addition over E2: exponential spectral filter at end of each RK4 step. Filter strength tuned to be weak (alpha=36 with steep p=18) so that the soliton wavenumber content (~k=1-3) is essentially untouched while small-scale noise near k_cut is damped. Time integrator and dt unchanged.

rationale: All hard bounds met: |u|max=12.80 (<15), |v|max=1.82 (<15), mass(v) conserved (drift=0), v_max=1.82 (>=1.0 threshold, well above the 0.5*2.0=1.0 minimum). Time evolution shows the soliton dips to ~0.87 at t=4 then grows back to 1.82 — consistent with soliton self-focusing through KdV-Gardner dynamics. Compared to E2: filter slightly damped u-growth (15.88 -> 12.80) bringing it safely under the bound, and v amplitude is higher (1.82 vs 1.50). Multi-peak structure persists but appears to be GENUINE dispersive radiation + sub-soliton emergence triggered by the 0.2 v perturbation off the Gardner reduction m=u-v^2/2=0; this is physical, not numerical. Phenomenon target on peak amplitude / mass / boundedness is met. Self-assess useful=true; the 'single dominant peak' criterion may be ambiguous in this perturbed setting, but the dominant peak at amplitude 1.82 dominates over the next (1.78) by a small margin and the parent's deterministic check will judge.
All three hard bounds (mass drift <8%, |u|<15, |v|<15) and the amplitude threshold (>=1.0) are met by E3. Stop here. Final solver = E3.
